title: PCOS Canonical Schema
description: Open canonical schema for harmonizing cross-sectional PCOS research datasets. Organized around the Rotterdam 2003 criteria. Draft; not clinically validated.

missing_code.description: Typed missingness. Missing values are never blank.

provenance.description: Emitted for every populated field. source_column_name is required when value_source is 'source'; transformation_applied records the formula and inputs when it is 'computed'.

value_source.description: Whether the value was mapped from a source column or computed from other canonical fields via x_fallback.

source_dataset.description: Dataset the value originated from. Carried per value rather than per row so that merged multi-source datasets remain traceable.

race_ethnicity.x_note: Preserve source label verbatim; do not recode.

waist_circumference_cm.x_loinc_display: Waist Circumference at umbilicus by Tape measure

x_fallback.source: WHO, Waist Circumference and Waist-Hip Ratio, 2008

hormonal_contraceptive_use.x_note: Suppresses androgens and imposes artificial cycles. Absence invalidates most androgen and cycle analysis.

contraceptive_type.x_note: Suppression magnitude differs by type. Combined oral contraceptives raise SHBG and suppress androgens most strongly; copper IUD and non-hormonal methods do not. Preserve the source label in value_raw.

pregnancy_status.x_note: Invalidates most hormonal and cycle fields.

oophorectomy_flag.x_note: Bilateral removal. Androgen and morphology fields become not_applicable.

antiandrogen_use.x_note: e.g. spironolactone. Suppresses clinical and biochemical androgen signals.

cycle_length_variability_days.x_note: SD or range; record which in transformation_applied.

total_testosterone.x_note: Unit disambiguation: values ~0.5-3 suggest nmol/L; ~15-90 suggest ng/dL.

shbg.x_loinc_display: Sex hormone binding globulin [Moles/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

warnings.message: SHBG raised by combined oral contraceptives; FAI is uninterpretable

dheas.x_loinc_display: Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

androgen_assay_method.x_note: Immunoassay and LC-MS/MS are not comparable at female concentrations. Pooling without this field produces confidently wrong results.

hirsutism_flag.x_note: Threshold varies by ethnicity; record applied threshold in transformation_applied.

antral_follicle_count_max.x_note: Per-ovary maximum. This is the criterion value.

x_fallback.source: Field selection; the Rotterdam criterion is stated per ovary
Field selection; the Rotterdam criterion is stated per ovary

ultrasound_route.x_note: Resolution differs materially; affects valid follicle-count threshold.

amh.x_note: May substitute for ultrasound in adults per 2023 International Guideline.
amh.x_loinc_display: Mullerian inhibiting substance [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

pcom_threshold_applied.x_note: e.g. 'AFC>=12', 'AFC>=20', 'volume>10ml'. Threshold moved from >=12 to >=20 as ultrasound resolution improved; a bare boolean is not comparable across eras.

lh.x_loinc_display: Luteinizing hormone [Units/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

lh_fsh_ratio.x_note: Historically used; no longer a diagnostic criterion.

tsh.x_note: Thyroid dysfunction — an exclusion for PCOS.

free_t4.x_loinc_display: Thyroxine (T4) free [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

17_hydroxyprogesterone.x_note: Non-classic congenital adrenal hyperplasia — an exclusion for PCOS.

exclusions_assessed_flag.x_note: Without this, a positive PCOS label cannot be distinguished from an unworked-up PCOS-like presentation.

fasting_glucose.x_note: Unit disambiguation: values ~4-7 suggest mmol/L; ~70-130 suggest mg/dL.
fasting_glucose.x_loinc_display: Fasting glucose [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

ogtt_2hr_glucose.x_loinc_display: Glucose [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma --2 hours post dose glucose

hdl_cholesterol.x_loinc_display: Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma

ldl_cholesterol.x_loinc_display: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/Vol] in Serum or Plasma by calculation

depression_screen_score.x_note: Raw instrument score. Scales differ in range and direction; not comparable across instruments.

depression_instrument.x_note: Required if depression_screen_score present. Use 'other' for unlisted instruments and preserve the verbatim name in value_raw.

anxiety_instrument.x_note: Required if anxiety_screen_score present. Use 'other' for unlisted instruments and preserve the verbatim name in value_raw.

quality_of_life_instrument.x_note: Required if quality_of_life_score present. PCOSQ and variants are PCOS-specific; the others are generic.

diagnostic_criteria_version.x_note: Criteria differ substantively; without this, diagnosis flags are not comparable across datasets.

phenotype.x_note: Phenotypes differ in metabolic risk; collapsing to a single flag loses most of the signal.

cycle_day_at_draw.x_note: Days since menses onset. Frequently undefined in this population — expected, not an error.

fasting_status.x_note: Gates interpretability of glucose, insulin, and lipid values.

_provenance.description: Map of canonical field name to its provenance record.

x_coverage_report.description: Fields the coverage report must evaluate.
x_coverage_report.verdict_rule: A dataset can support a Rotterdam case definition only if at least 2 of 3 criteria have evaluable evidence AND exclusions_assessed_flag is populated. Evaluating the criteria themselves is the tool's job, not the schema's.

x_validator_rules: If age_years - age_at_menarche < 3 and ovulatory dysfunction is inferred from cycle irregularity alone, emit warning.
If hysterectomy_flag is true, cycle fields must be not_applicable rather than missing.
If hormonal_contraceptive_use = current, androgen and cycle fields must carry a suppression warning.
If hormonal_contraceptive_use = never or none, contraceptive_type must be 'none', 'non_hormonal', or missing — not a hormonal type.
If contraceptive_type = combined_oral and hormonal_contraceptive_use = current, escalate the androgen suppression warning: SHBG elevation makes free_androgen_index uninterpretable.
If any androgen value is present and androgen_assay_method is missing, emit critical warning.
If depression_screen_score is present, depression_instrument must be present.
If anxiety_screen_score is present, anxiety_instrument must be present.
If quality_of_life_score is present, quality_of_life_instrument must be present.
If any instrument field = 'other', value_raw must carry the verbatim instrument name.
Screen scores are never harmonized across instruments; pooling scores with differing instrument values must emit a warning.
If a source-supplied value and its computed equivalent disagree beyond rounding tolerance, emit a warning. The source value is kept.
Unit conversion is performed by deterministic code, never by the LLM; the LLM only infers unit_raw.
If unit_raw cannot be inferred, conversion and all dependent derivations are blocked until a reviewer supplies it.
Values outside x_range emit a physiological-plausibility warning, not a hard failure — they may indicate an unconverted unit.
A field is computed only when no source column mapped to it and every input declared in x_fallback.inputs is present in the unit that input specifies.
value_source must be 'source' whenever a source column mapped to the field, even if the value could also have been computed.
If pcos_diagnosis_flag or phenotype is populated, diagnosis_source and diagnostic_criteria_version must also be populated — these fields record what a source reported, never a computed result.
If pcom_threshold_applied is populated, the source must also supply the morphology values it was applied to.

x_field_ids.description: Every field carries a frozen x_id. Ids are 1-based. From v0.1 publication onward they are permanent: never reassigned, never reused. New fields take the next unused integer regardless of alphabetical position. Removed fields leave their id retired so that mapping files written against earlier versions continue to resolve correctly.

x_terminology.units: x_unit is the human-readable canonical unit; x_unit_ucum is the same unit as a UCUM code (https://ucum.org). Use x_unit_ucum for machine processing.
x_terminology.loinc: x_loinc gives the LOINC code for laboratory and measurement fields (https://loinc.org). Codes marked x_loinc_verified: false are provisional and must be confirmed against the LOINC database before use.
x_terminology.loinc_caveat: LOINC codes distinguish mass-based from molar-based measurements. Codes here name the analyte; the canonical unit in x_unit governs. Where a source reports a different scale, the LOINC code may differ from the one listed even though the analyte matches.

not_adopted.SNOMED_CT: Clinical findings (hirsutism, acne, PCOS diagnosis) have SNOMED concepts but licensing and the small closed concept set made adoption low-value for v0.1.
not_adopted.CDISC_SDTM: Trials-oriented; wrong shape for observational research data.
not_adopted.HL7_FHIR: Exchange format, not a tabular research schema. A FHIR projection could be added later.

x_derivation.description: A field may be populated from a source column or, if absent, computed from other canonical fields. Both are the same field: population method is recorded in the provenance record, not by a separate field.
x_derivation.precedence: A source-supplied value always wins. A computed value is used only when the source has none.
x_derivation.scope: x_fallback is given only where the calculation is a published standard or plain field selection, cited in x_fallback.source. This schema defines no diagnostic algorithm: criteria evaluation, phenotype assignment, and case definition are interpretations of published guidelines and belong in tools, not in the schema.
x_derivation.reported_diagnoses: pcos_diagnosis_flag, phenotype, and criteria_met carry what a source reported, together with diagnostic_criteria_version and diagnosis_source. They are observations, never computed here.

x_fallback_spec.description: Machine-readable fallback computations. A field carrying x_fallback may be computed when no source column mapped to it. Source-supplied values always take precedence.

ops.expression: Evaluate `expression` with each input bound to its field name. Arithmetic only: + - * / ** and parentheses. No function calls.
ops.max: Maximum of the input values. Null if ANY input is missing — a partial maximum is not the same quantity.
ops.min: Minimum of the input values. Same missing-input rule as max.

contract.inputs: Each input names a canonical field and the unit the expression assumes. Convert to that unit before binding; it is not always the field's canonical unit (see homa_ir).
contract.guards: All guards must pass or the field is not computed. Conditions: gt, gte, lt, lte, eq, ne.
contract.warnings: Emitted alongside the computed value; they do not block computation.
contract.requires_all_inputs: Compute only when every input is present. A missing input yields not_measured, never a partial result.
contract.output_unit: UCUM code of the result. Must equal the field's x_unit_ucum.
